title: Hypertension
details: Hypertension is a chronic condition involving elevated blood pressure requiring monitoring and lifestyle adjustments.

title: Type 2 Diabetes
details: Type 2 diabetes involves insulin resistance and is managed with diet, medication, and monitoring.

title: Asthma
details: Asthma causes airway inflammation and difficulty breathing, often triggered by allergens or exertion.

title: COPD
details: COPD includes chronic bronchitis and emphysema, leading to airflow limitation.

title: Fever Management
details: Fever is commonly treated with hydration, rest, and antipyretics if necessary.

title: Wound Care
details: Clean wound with sterile solution, apply dressing, monitor for infection.

title: Infection Control
details: Hand hygiene and PPE are critical for infection prevention.

title: Blood Pressure Monitoring
details: Measure regularly using a calibrated device, record readings for trends.

title: Pulse Measurement
details: Pulse can be measured at wrist or neck, normal range varies by age.

title: Respiration Rate
details: Normal adult respiration is 12-20 breaths per minute.

title: Fall Risk Assessment
details: Assess mobility, environment hazards, and medication effects.

title: Mobility Assistance
details: Use assistive devices and proper body mechanics.

title: Hydration Needs
details: Adequate hydration supports organ function and recovery.

title: Basic Nutrition
details: Balanced diet includes proteins, carbs, fats, vitamins, and minerals.

title: Pain Management
details: Pain can be managed with medication, positioning, and therapy.

title: Medication Administration
details: Follow the 5 rights: right patient, drug, dose, route, time.

title: Medication Errors
details: Double-check prescriptions and patient identity.

title: Medication Adherence
details: Use reminders and simplify schedules.

title: Mental Health Support
details: Support includes communication, therapy, and medication when needed.

title: Anxiety
details: Symptoms include restlessness, tension, and rapid heart rate.

title: Depression
details: Persistent sadness and loss of interest in activities.

title: Elderly Care
details: Focus on mobility, safety, and chronic condition management.

title: Pediatric Care
details: Children require age-specific care and monitoring.

title: Post-Operative Care
details: Monitor vitals, manage pain, prevent infection.

title: Pre-Operative Prep
details: Ensure patient readiness and informed consent.

title: Oxygen Therapy
details: Used for patients with low oxygen saturation.

title: IV Therapy
details: Delivers fluids and medication directly into bloodstream.

title: Cardiac Monitoring
details: Monitor ECG, heart rate, and rhythm.

title: Stroke Awareness
details: FAST: Face droop, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to act.

title: Allergy Management
details: Avoid triggers and use antihistamines when needed.

title: Skin Rash
details: May result from allergy, infection, or irritation.

title: Sleep Health
details: Adequate sleep improves healing and mental health.

title: Fatigue
details: May result from illness, stress, or poor sleep.

title: Exercise Benefits
details: Improves cardiovascular health and overall wellbeing.

title: Rehabilitation
details: Includes physical and occupational therapy.

title: Triage Basics
details: Assess severity to determine treatment priority.

title: Emergency Response
details: Stabilize patient and call emergency services if needed.

title: Clinical Documentation
details: Accurate records are essential for care continuity.

title: Patient Privacy
details: Maintain confidentiality and secure records.
